# Argus

> Argus is a multi-provider search broker for AI agents. Routes queries across SearXNG, Brave, Serper, Tavily, and Exa with automatic fallback, RRF ranking, health tracking, and budget enforcement. Extract clean text from any URL. Multi-turn sessions with context-enriched follow-ups. API key auth. SQLite only — zero external database dependencies. Connect via HTTP, CLI, MCP, or Python.

## What It Does

> You pass Argus a search query. It fans out to multiple providers, collects results, ranks them via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF with k=60), deduplicates by URL, and returns a clean list. If a provider is down or over budget, it skips it automatically. Your project never touches a provider API directly.

> The output is designed for LLM consumption — enough context (title, snippet, URL, domain, relevance score) for a model to decide "this link is worth reading" without downloading the whole page.

> Content extraction fetches clean article text from any URL (trafilatura primary, Jina fallback), cached in memory and SQLite. Multi-turn sessions remember prior queries for smarter follow-ups.

## Installation

> git clone https://github.com/Khamel83/argus.git && cd argus; python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate; pip install -e ".[mcp]"; argus serve

## Provider Setup

> All you need is API keys for whichever providers you want. SearXNG is free and runs locally. The rest have generous free tiers.

> | Provider | Free tier | Get a key |
> |----------|----------|-----------|
> | SearXNG | Unlimited (self-hosted) | No key needed |
> | Brave Search | 2,000 queries/month | https://brave.com/search/api/ |
> | Serper | 2,500 queries/month | https://serper.dev |
> | Tavily | 1,000 queries/month | https://tavily.com |
> | Exa | 1,000 queries/month | https://exa.ai |

> Set keys in .env: ARGUS_BRAVE_API_KEY=, ARGUS_SERPER_API_KEY=, ARGUS_TAVILY_API_KEY=, ARGUS_EXA_API_KEY=. Unset or blank keys are silently skipped.

## Integration

### HTTP API

> POST /api/search with {"query": "...", "mode": "discovery", "max_results": 5}. POST /api/extract for content extraction. POST /api/recover-url for dead URL recovery. Set ARGUS_API_KEY to require authentication (health exempt). OpenAPI docs at /docs.

### CLI

> argus search -q "query" --mode discovery; argus search -q "query" --mode research -n 20; argus extract -u "https://example.com/article"; argus recover-url -u "https://dead.link"; argus health; argus budgets; argus test-provider -p brave

### MCP

> Start with `argus mcp serve` (stdio) or `argus mcp serve --transport sse --port 8001` (HTTP). Tools: search_web, extract_content, recover_url, expand_links, search_health, search_budgets, test_provider.

> Claude Code config: {"mcpServers": {"argus": {"command": "argus", "args": ["mcp", "serve"]}}}

### Python

> from argus.broker.router import create_broker; from argus.models import SearchQuery, SearchMode; from argus.extraction import extract_url; broker = create_broker(); response = await broker.search(SearchQuery(query="...", mode=SearchMode.DISCOVERY)); content = await extract_url(response.results[0].url)

## Search Modes

> discovery: Find related pages and canonical sources. Chain: searxng → brave → exa → tavily → serper.
> recovery: Find a moved or dead URL. Chain: searxng → brave → serper → tavily → exa.
> grounding: Few live sources for fact-checking. Chain: brave → serper → searxng.
> research: Broad exploratory retrieval. Chain: tavily → exa → brave → serper.

## Configuration

> All config via environment variables with ARGUS_ prefix. Key variables: ARGUS_DB_PATH (SQLite — all tables in one file), ARGUS_API_KEY (auth), provider API keys (ARGUS_BRAVE_API_KEY, etc.), ARGUS_CACHE_TTL_HOURS (default 168), ARGUS_RATE_LIMIT (default 60 req/min), ARGUS_JINA_API_KEY (extraction fallback).

## Architecture

> Caller (CLI/HTTP/MCP/Python) → SearchBroker → routing policy (per mode) → providers (cheap-first, with fallback) → cache (TTLCache) → dedupe → RRF ranking → response → SQLite persistence (non-fatal). ContentExtractor: trafilatura → Jina, cached in memory + SQLite. Generic TTLCache and SlidingWindowLimiter in argus/core/. No external database dependencies.

## Documentation

> README.md — full documentation with quick start, integration examples, configuration
> docs/providers.md — provider setup and budget configuration
> docs/routing-contract.md — authoritative routing, fallback, and ranking spec
> CONTRIBUTING.md — contribution guide and how to add providers
